seoTitle: Paraguay Residency by Investment | Investor Pass
seoDescription: Get permanent residency in Paraguay through investment from USD 70,000. Routes to residency and a path to citizenship by naturalisation. Guidance from Lincoln.
heroEyebrow: República del Paraguay · Residency by Investment
heroLead: A single qualifying investment grants direct permanent residency in one of South America's fastest-growing economies. Established under Resolution N° 0283/2026, the Investor Pass bypasses the temporary-residency stage entirely — with a clear pathway, in time, to citizenship by naturalisation.

faqs.question: What is the Paraguay Investor Pass?
faqs.answer: It is Paraguay's investment-linked residency framework, established by Resolution N° 0283/2026. A qualifying investment through an approved route gives you access to the Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) and the permanent residency process — and, after the constitutional residence period, the right to apply for citizenship by naturalisation.

faqs.question: How much do I need to invest?
faqs.answer: Resolution 0283/2026 sets four routes: a company capitalised through SUACE from USD 70,000, approved tourism projects from USD 150,000, real estate from USD 200,000, and financial instruments from USD 200,000. A separate, pre-existing pathway via Paraguay's audiovisual institute allows residency through a film-production contribution from approximately USD 40,000. We help you choose the route that matches your strategy, not just your budget.

faqs.question: Is the Foreign Investor Certificate the same as residency?
faqs.answer: No. The MIC must issue the Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) within five business days of a complete application, but it is a distinct investor credential. Permanent residency is a separate status granted by the migration authority, with its own process and timeline. We keep the two clearly apart — and so does the law.

faqs.question: When can I apply for Paraguayan citizenship?
faqs.answer: Paraguay's Constitution (Art. 148) permits an application for naturalisation after three years of permanent residence, subject to language, civics, and physical-presence requirements, with further administrative processing time. Citizenship is granted through naturalisation — it is not conferred by the investment itself.

faqs.question: Will Paraguay tax my worldwide income?
faqs.answer: Paraguay applies a territorial tax system, so income arising outside Paraguay is generally outside the scope of Paraguayan income tax. Legal residency and tax residency are separate concepts: holding an Investor Pass does not by itself determine where you are tax resident. We assess that individually, within your wider structure.

faqs.question: Can I include my family?
faqs.answer: Family members can generally be included in an Investor Pass application. The specifics depend on your circumstances and the route chosen, and are confirmed before anything is committed.
